36	
37	def pad_to_len(
38	    arr: torch.tensor,
39	    target_len: int,
40	    *,
41	    left_pad: bool,
42	    eos_token: int,
43	    device: torch.device,
44	) -> torch.tensor:
45	  """Pad or truncate array to given length."""
46	  if arr.shape[1] < target_len:
47	    shape_for_ones = list(arr.shape)
48	    shape_for_ones[1] = target_len - shape_for_ones[1]
49	    padded = (
50	        torch.ones(
51	            shape_for_ones,
52	            device=device,
53	            dtype=torch.long,
54	        )
55	        * eos_token
56	    )
57	    if not left_pad:
58	      return torch.concatenate((arr, padded), dim=1)
59	    else:
60	      return torch.concatenate((padded, arr), dim=1)
61	  else:
62	    return arr[:, :target_len]
63	
64	
65	def filter_and_truncate(
66	    outputs: torch.tensor,
67	    truncation_length: Optional[int],
68	    eos_token_mask: torch.tensor,
69	) -> torch.tensor:
70	  """Filter and truncate outputs to given length.
71	
72	  Args:
73	   outputs: output tensor of shape [batch_size, output_len]
74	   truncation_length: Length to truncate the final output. If None, then no
75	     truncation is applied.
76	   eos_token_mask: EOS token mask of shape [batch_size, output_len]
77	
78	  Returns:
79	   output tensor of shape [batch_size, truncation_length].
80	  """
81	  if truncation_length:
82	    outputs = outputs[:, :truncation_length]
83	    truncation_mask = torch.sum(eos_token_mask, dim=1) >= truncation_length
84	    return outputs[truncation_mask, :]
85	  return outputs
86	
87	
88	def process_outputs_for_training(
89	    all_outputs: Sequence[torch.Tensor],
90	    logits_processor: logits_processing.SynthIDLogitsProcessor,
91	    tokenizer: Any,
92	    *,
93	    pos_truncation_length: Optional[int],
94	    neg_truncation_length: Optional[int],
95	    max_length: int,
96	    is_cv: bool,
97	    is_pos: bool,
98	    torch_device: torch.device,
99	) -> tuple[Sequence[torch.tensor], Sequence[torch.tensor]]:
100	  """Process raw model outputs into format understandable by the detector.
101	
102	  Args:
103	   all_outputs: sequence of outputs of shape [batch_size, output_len].
104	   logits_processor: logits processor used for watermarking.
105	   tokenizer: tokenizer used for the model.
106	   pos_truncation_length: Length to truncate the watermarked outputs. If None,
107	     then no truncation is applied.
108	   neg_truncation_length: Length to truncate the unwatermarked outputs. If None,
109	     then no truncation is applied.
110	   max_length: Length to pad truncated outputs so that all processed entries.
111	     have same shape.
112	   is_cv: Process given outputs for cross validation.
113	   is_pos: Process given outputs for positives.
114	   torch_device: torch device to use.
115	
116	  Returns:
117	    Tuple of
118	      all_masks: list of masks of shape [batch_size, max_length].
119	      all_g_values: list of g_values of shape [batch_size, max_length, depth].
120	  """
121	  all_masks = []
122	  all_g_values = []
123	  for outputs in tqdm.tqdm(all_outputs):
124	    # outputs is of shape [batch_size, output_len].
125	    # output_len can differ from batch to batch.
126	    eos_token_mask = logits_processor.compute_eos_token_mask(
127	        input_ids=outputs,
128	        eos_token_id=tokenizer.eos_token_id,
129	    )
130	    if is_pos or is_cv:
131	      # filter with length for positives for both train and CV.
132	      # We also filter for length when CV negatives are processed.
133	      outputs = filter_and_truncate(
134	          outputs, pos_truncation_length, eos_token_mask
135	      )
136	    elif not is_pos and not is_cv:
137	      outputs = filter_and_truncate(
138	          outputs, neg_truncation_length, eos_token_mask
139	      )
140	
141	    # If no filtered outputs skip this batch.
142	    if outputs.shape[0] == 0:
143	      continue
144	
145	    # All outputs are padded to max-length with eos-tokens.
146	    outputs = pad_to_len(
147	        outputs,
148	        max_length,
149	        left_pad=False,
150	        eos_token=tokenizer.eos_token_id,
151	        device=torch_device,
152	    )
153	    # outputs shape [num_filtered_entries, max_length]
154	
155	    eos_token_mask = logits_processor.compute_eos_token_mask(
156	        input_ids=outputs,
157	        eos_token_id=tokenizer.eos_token_id,
158	    )
159	
160	    context_repetition_mask = logits_processor.compute_context_repetition_mask(
161	        input_ids=outputs,
162	    )
163	
164	    # context_repetition_mask of shape [num_filtered_entries, max_length -
165	    # (ngram_len - 1)].
166	    context_repetition_mask = pad_to_len(
167	        context_repetition_mask,
168	        max_length,
169	        left_pad=True,
170	        eos_token=0,
171	        device=torch_device,
172	    )
173	    # We pad on left to get same max_length shape.
174	    # context_repetition_mask of shape [num_filtered_entries, max_length].
175	    combined_mask = context_repetition_mask * eos_token_mask
176	
177	    g_values = logits_processor.compute_g_values(
178	        input_ids=outputs,
179	    )
180	
181	    # g_values of shape [num_filtered_entries, max_length - (ngram_len - 1),
182	    # depth].
183	    g_values = pad_to_len(
184	        g_values, max_length, left_pad=True, eos_token=0, device=torch_device
185	    )
186	
187	    # We pad on left to get same max_length shape.
188	    # g_values of shape [num_filtered_entries, max_length, depth].
189	    all_masks.append(combined_mask)
190	    all_g_values.append(g_values)
191	  return all_masks, all_g_values
192	
193	
194	@enum.unique
195	class ScoreType(enum.Enum):
196	  """Type of score returned by a WatermarkDetector.
197	
198	  In all cases, larger score corresponds to watermarked text.
199	  """
200	
201	  # Negative p-value where the p-value is the probability of observing equal or
202	  # stronger watermarking in unwatermarked text.
203	  NEGATIVE_P_VALUE = enum.auto()
204	
205	  # Prob(watermarked | g-values).
206	  POSTERIOR = enum.auto()
207	
208	
209	class LikelihoodModel(abc.ABC):
210	  """Watermark likelihood model base class defining __call__ interface."""
211	
212	  @abc.abstractmethod
213	  def __call__(self, g_values: jnp.ndarray) -> jnp.ndarray:
214	    """Computes likelihoods given g-values and a mask.
215	
216	    Args:
217	      g_values: g-values (all are 0 or 1) of shape [batch_size, seq_len,
218	        watermarking_depth, ...].
219	
220	    Returns:
221	      an array of shape [batch_size, seq_len, watermarking_depth] or
222	      [batch_size, seq_len, 1] corresponding to the likelihoods
223	      of the g-values given either the watermarked hypothesis or
224	      the unwatermarked hypothesis; i.e. either P(g|watermarked)
225	      or P(g|unwatermarked).
226	    """
227	
228	
229	class LikelihoodModelWatermarked(nn.Module, LikelihoodModel):
230	  """Watermarked likelihood model for binary-valued g-values.
231	
232	  This takes in g-values and returns P(g_values|watermarked).
233	  """
234	
235	  watermarking_depth: int
236	  params: Optional[Mapping[str, Mapping[str, Any]]] = None
237	
238	  def setup(self):
239	    """Initializes the model parameters."""
240	
241	    def noise(seed, shape):
242	      return jax.random.normal(key=jax.random.PRNGKey(seed), shape=shape)
243	
244	    self.beta = self.param(
245	        "beta",
246	        lambda *x: (
247	            -2.5 + 0.001 * noise(seed=0, shape=(1, 1, self.watermarking_depth))
248	        ),
249	    )
250	    self.delta = self.param(
251	        "delta",
252	        lambda *x: (
253	            0.001
254	            * noise(
255	                seed=0,
256	                shape=(1, 1, self.watermarking_depth, self.watermarking_depth),
257	            )
258	        ),
259	    )
260	
261	  def l2_loss(self) -> jnp.ndarray:
262	    return jnp.einsum("ijkl->", self.delta**2)
263	
264	  def _compute_latents(
265	      self, g_values: jnp.ndarray
266	  ) -> tuple[jnp.ndarray, jnp.ndarray]:
267	    """Computes the unique token probability distribution given g-values.
268	
269	    Args:
270	      g_values: Pseudorandom function values of shape [batch_size, seq_len,
271	        watermarking_depth].
272	
273	    Returns:
274	      p_one_unique_token and p_two_unique_tokens, both of shape
275	        [batch_size, seq_len, watermarking_depth]. p_one_unique_token[i,t,l]
276	        gives the probability of there being one unique token in a tournament
277	        match on layer l, on timestep t, for batch item i.
278	        p_one_unique_token[i,t,l] + p_two_unique_token[i,t,l] = 1.
279	    """
280	    # Tile g-values to produce feature vectors for predicting the latents
281	    # for each layer in the tournament; our model for the latents psi is a
282	    # logistic regression model psi = sigmoid(delta * x + beta).
283	    x = jnp.repeat(
284	        jnp.expand_dims(g_values, axis=-2), self.watermarking_depth, axis=-2
285	    )  # [batch_size, seq_len, watermarking_depth, watermarking_depth]
286	
287	    x = jnp.tril(
288	        x, k=-1
289	    )  # mask all elements above -1 diagonal for autoregressive factorization
290	
291	    logits = (
292	        jnp.einsum("ijkl,ijkl->ijk", self.delta, x) + self.beta
293	    )  # [batch_size, seq_len, watermarking_depth]
294	
295	    p_two_unique_tokens = jax.nn.sigmoid(logits)
296	    p_one_unique_token = 1 - p_two_unique_tokens
297	    return p_one_unique_token, p_two_unique_tokens
298	
299	  def __call__(self, g_values: jnp.ndarray) -> jnp.ndarray:
300	    """Computes the likelihoods P(g_values|watermarked).
301	
302	    Args:
303	      g_values: g-values (values 0 or 1) of shape [batch_size, seq_len,
304	        watermarking_depth]
305	
306	    Returns:
307	      p(g_values|watermarked) of shape [batch_size, seq_len,
308	      watermarking_depth].
309	    """
310	    p_one_unique_token, p_two_unique_tokens = self._compute_latents(g_values)
311	
312	    # P(g_tl | watermarked) is equal to
313	    # 0.5 * [ (g_tl+0.5) * p_two_unique_tokens + p_one_unique_token].
314	    return 0.5 * ((g_values + 0.5) * p_two_unique_tokens + p_one_unique_token)
315	
316	
317	class LikelihoodModelUnwatermarked(nn.Module, LikelihoodModel):
318	  """Unwatermarked likelihood model for binary-valued g-values.
319	
320	  This takes in g-values and returns p(g_values | not watermarked).
321	  """
322	
323	  @nn.compact
324	  def __call__(self, g_values: jnp.ndarray) -> jnp.ndarray:
325	    """Computes the likelihoods P(g-values|not watermarked).
326	
327	    Args:
328	      g_values: g-values (0 or 1 values) of shape [batch_size, seq_len,
329	        watermarking_depth, ...].
330	
331	    Returns:
332	      Likelihoods of g-values given text is unwatermarked --
333	      p(g_values | not watermarked) of shape [batch_size, seq_len,
334	      watermarking_depth].
335	    """
336	    return 0.5 * jnp.ones_like(g_values)  # all g-values have prob 0.5.
337	
338	
339	def _compute_posterior(
340	    likelihoods_watermarked: jnp.ndarray,
341	    likelihoods_unwatermarked: jnp.ndarray,
342	    mask: jnp.ndarray,
343	    prior: float,
344	) -> jnp.ndarray:
345	  """Compute posterior P(w|g) given likelihoods, mask and prior.
346	
347	  Args:
348	    likelihoods_watermarked: shape [batch, length, depth]. Likelihoods
349	      P(g_values|watermarked) of g-values under watermarked model.
350	    likelihoods_unwatermarked: shape [batch, length, depth]. Likelihoods
351	      P(g_values|unwatermarked) of g-values under unwatermarked model.
352	    mask: A binary array shape [batch, length] indicating which g-values should
353	      be used. g-values with mask value 0 are discarded.
354	    prior: Prior probability P(w) that the text is watermarked.
355	
356	  Returns:
357	    Posterior probability P(watermarked|g_values), shape [batch].
358	  """
359	  mask = jnp.expand_dims(mask, -1)
360	  prior = jnp.clip(prior, a_min=1e-5, a_max=1 - 1e-5)
361	  log_likelihoods_watermarked = jnp.log(
362	      jnp.clip(likelihoods_watermarked, a_min=1e-30, a_max=float("inf"))
363	  )
364	  log_likelihoods_unwatermarked = jnp.log(
365	      jnp.clip(likelihoods_unwatermarked, a_min=1e-30, a_max=float("inf"))
366	  )
367	  log_odds = log_likelihoods_watermarked - log_likelihoods_unwatermarked
368	
369	  # Sum relative surprisals (log odds) across all token positions and layers.
370	  relative_surprisal_likelihood = jnp.einsum(
371	      "i...->i", log_odds * mask
372	  )  # [batch_size].
373	
374	  relative_surprisal_prior = jnp.log(prior) - jnp.log(1 - prior)
375	
376	  # Combine prior and likelihood.
377	  relative_surprisal = (
378	      relative_surprisal_prior + relative_surprisal_likelihood
379	  )  # [batch_size]
380	
381	  # Compute the posterior probability P(w|g) = sigmoid(relative_surprisal).
382	  return jax.nn.sigmoid(relative_surprisal)
383	
384	
385	class BayesianDetectorModule(nn.Module):
386	  """Bayesian classifier for watermark detection Flax Module.
387	
388	  This detector uses Bayes' rule to compute a watermarking score, which is
389	  the posterior probability P(watermarked|g_values) that the text is
390	  watermarked, given its g_values.
391	
392	  Note that this detector only works with Tournament-based watermarking using
393	  the Bernoulli(0.5) g-value distribution.
394	  """
395	
396	  watermarking_depth: int  # The number of tournament layers.
397	  params: Optional[Mapping[str, Mapping[str, Any]]] = None
398	  baserate: float = 0.5  # Prior probability P(w) that a text is watermarked.
399	
400	  @property
401	  def score_type(self) -> ScoreType:
402	    return ScoreType.POSTERIOR
403	
404	  def l2_loss(self) -> jnp.ndarray:
405	    return self.likelihood_model_watermarked.l2_loss()
406	
407	  def setup(self):
408	    """Initializes the model parameters."""
409	
410	    def _fetch_params():
411	      return {"params:": self.params["params"]["likelihood_model_watermarked"]}
412	
413	    self.likelihood_model_watermarked = LikelihoodModelWatermarked(
414	        watermarking_depth=self.watermarking_depth,
415	        params=_fetch_params() if self.params is not None else None,
416	    )
417	    self.likelihood_model_unwatermarked = LikelihoodModelUnwatermarked()
418	    self.prior = self.param("prior", lambda *x: self.baserate, (1,))
419	
420	  def __call__(
421	      self,
422	      g_values: jnp.ndarray,
423	      mask: jnp.ndarray,
424	  ) -> jnp.ndarray:
425	    """Computes the watermarked posterior P(watermarked|g_values).
426	
427	    Args:
428	      g_values: g-values (with values 0 or 1) of shape [batch_size, seq_len,
429	        watermarking_depth, ...]
430	      mask: A binary array shape [batch_size, seq_len] indicating which g-values
431	        should be used. g-values with mask value 0 are discarded.
432	
433	    Returns:
434	      P(watermarked | g_values), of shape [batch_size].
435	    """
436	
437	    likelihoods_watermarked = self.likelihood_model_watermarked(g_values)
438	    likelihoods_unwatermarked = self.likelihood_model_unwatermarked(g_values)
439	    return _compute_posterior(
440	        likelihoods_watermarked, likelihoods_unwatermarked, mask, self.prior
441	    )
442	
443	  def score(
444	      self,
445	      g_values: Union[jnp.ndarray, Sequence[jnp.ndarray]],
446	      mask: jnp.ndarray,
447	  ) -> jnp.ndarray:
448	    if self.params is None:
449	      raise ValueError("params must be set before calling score")
450	    return self.apply(self.params, g_values, mask, method=self.__call__)
451	
452	
453	def xentropy_loss(y: jnp.ndarray, y_pred: jnp.ndarray) -> jnp.ndarray:
454	  """Calculates cross entropy loss."""
455	  y_pred = jnp.clip(y_pred, 1e-5, 1 - 1e-5)
456	  return -jnp.mean((y * jnp.log(y_pred) + (1 - y) * jnp.log(1 - y_pred)))
457	
458	
459	def loss_fn(
460	    params: Mapping[str, Any],
461	    detector_inputs: Any,
462	    w_true: jnp.ndarray,
463	    l2_batch_weight: float,
464	    detector_module: BayesianDetectorModule,
465	) -> jnp.ndarray:
466	  """Calculates loss for a batch of data given parameters."""
467	  w_pred = detector_module.apply(
468	      params, *detector_inputs, method=detector_module.__call__
469	  )
470	  unweighted_l2 = detector_module.apply(params, method=detector_module.l2_loss)
471	  l2_loss = l2_batch_weight * unweighted_l2
472	  return xentropy_loss(w_true, w_pred) + l2_loss
473	
474	
475	def tpr_at_fpr(
476	    params: Mapping[str, Any],
477	    detector_inputs: Any,
478	    w_true: jnp.ndarray,
479	    minibatch_size,
480	    detector_module: BayesianDetectorModule,
481	    target_fpr: float = 0.01,
482	) -> jnp.ndarray:
483	  """Calculates TPR at FPR=target_fpr."""
484	  positive_idxs = w_true == 1
485	  negative_idxs = w_true == 0
486	  inds = jnp.arange(0, len(detector_inputs[0]), minibatch_size)
487	  w_preds = []
488	  for start in inds:
489	    end = start + minibatch_size
490	    detector_inputs_ = (
491	        detector_inputs[0][start:end],
492	        detector_inputs[1][start:end],
493	    )
494	    w_pred = detector_module.apply(
495	        params, *detector_inputs_, method=detector_module.__call__
496	    )
497	    w_preds.append(w_pred)
498	  w_pred = jnp.concatenate(w_preds, axis=0)
499	  positive_scores = w_pred[positive_idxs]
500	  negative_scores = w_pred[negative_idxs]
501	  fpr_threshold = jnp.percentile(negative_scores, 100 - target_fpr * 100)
502	  return jnp.mean(positive_scores >= fpr_threshold)
503	
504	
505	@enum.unique
506	class ValidationMetric(enum.Enum):
507	  """Direction along the z-axis."""
508	
509	  TPR_AT_FPR = "tpr_at_fpr"
510	  CROSS_ENTROPY = "cross_entropy"
511	
512	
513	def train(
514	    *,
515	    detector_module: BayesianDetectorModule,
516	    g_values: jnp.ndarray,
517	    mask: jnp.ndarray,
518	    watermarked: jnp.ndarray,
519	    epochs: int = 250,
520	    learning_rate: float = 1e-3,
521	    minibatch_size: int = 64,
522	    seed: int = 0,
523	    l2_weight: float = 0.0,
524	    shuffle: bool = True,
525	    g_values_val: Optional[jnp.ndarray] = None,
526	    mask_val: Optional[jnp.ndarray] = None,
527	    watermarked_val: Optional[jnp.ndarray] = None,
528	    verbose: bool = False,
529	    validation_metric: ValidationMetric = ValidationMetric.TPR_AT_FPR,
530	) -> tuple[Mapping[int, Mapping[str, PyTree]], float]:
531	  """Trains a Bayesian detector model.
532	
533	  Args:
534	    detector_module: The detector module to train in-place.
535	    g_values: g-values of shape [num_train, seq_len, watermarking_depth].
536	    mask: A binary array shape [num_train, seq_len] indicating which g-values
537	      should be used. g-values with mask value 0 are discarded.
538	    watermarked: A binary array of shape [num_train] indicating whether the
539	      example is watermarked (0: unwatermarked, 1: watermarked).
540	    epochs: Number of epochs to train for.
541	    learning_rate: Learning rate for optimizer.
542	    minibatch_size: Minibatch size for training. Note that a minibatch requires
543	      ~ 32 * minibatch_size * seq_len * watermarked_depth * watermarked_depth
544	      bits of memory.
545	    seed: Seed for parameter initialization.
546	    l2_weight: Weight to apply to L2 regularization for delta parameters.
547	    shuffle: Whether to shuffle before training.
548	    g_values_val: Validation g-values of shape [num_val, seq_len,
549	      watermarking_depth].
550	    mask_val: Validation mask of shape [num_val, seq_len].
551	    watermarked_val: Validation watermark labels of shape [num_val].
552	    verbose: Boolean indicating verbosity of training. If true, the loss will be
553	      printed. Defaulted to False.
554	    validation_metric: validation metric to use.
555	
556	  Returns:
557	    Tuple of
558	      training_history: Training history keyed by epoch number where the
559	      values are
560	        dictionaries containing the loss, validation loss, and model
561	        parameters,
562	        keyed by
563	        'loss', 'val_loss', and 'params', respectively.
564	      min_val_loss: Minimum validation loss achieved during training.
565	  """
566	  minibatch_inds = jnp.arange(0, len(g_values), minibatch_size)
567	  minibatch_inds_val = None
568	  if g_values_val is not None:
569	    minibatch_inds_val = jnp.arange(0, len(g_values_val), minibatch_size)
570	
571	  rng = jax.random.PRNGKey(seed)
572	  param_rng, shuffle_rng = jax.random.split(rng)
573	
574	  def coshuffle(*args):
575	    return [jax.random.permutation(shuffle_rng, x) for x in args]
576	
577	  if shuffle:
578	    g_values, mask, watermarked = coshuffle(g_values, mask, watermarked)
579	
580	  def update_fn_if_fpr_tpr(params):
581	    """Loss function for negative TPR@FPR=1% as the validation loss."""
582	    tpr_ = tpr_at_fpr(
583	        params=params,
584	        detector_inputs=(g_values_val, mask_val),
585	        w_true=watermarked_val,
586	        minibatch_size=minibatch_size,
587	        detector_module=detector_module,
588	    )
589	    return -tpr_
590	
591	  n_minibatches = len(g_values) / minibatch_size
592	  l2_batch_weight_train = l2_weight / n_minibatches
593	  l2_batch_weight_val = 0.0
594	  loss_fn_train = functools.partial(
595	      loss_fn,
596	      l2_batch_weight=l2_batch_weight_train,
597	      detector_module=detector_module,
598	  )
599	  loss_fn_jitted_val = jax.jit(
600	      functools.partial(
601	          loss_fn,
602	          l2_batch_weight=l2_batch_weight_val,
603	          detector_module=detector_module,
604	      )
605	  )
606	
607	  @jax.jit
608	  def update(gvalues, masks, labels, params, opt_state):
609	    loss_fn_partialed = functools.partial(
610	        loss_fn_train,
611	        detector_inputs=(gvalues, masks),
612	        w_true=labels,
613	    )
614	    loss, grads = jax.value_and_grad(loss_fn_partialed)(params)
615	    updates, opt_state = optimizer.update(grads, opt_state)
616	    params = optax.apply_updates(params, updates)
617	    return loss, params, opt_state
618	
619	  def update_with_minibatches(gvalues, masks, labels, inds, params, opt_state):
620	    """Update params iff opt_state is not None and always returns the loss."""
621	    losses = []
622	    for start in inds:
623	      end = start + minibatch_size
624	      loss, params, opt_state = update(
625	          gvalues[start:end],
626	          masks[start:end],
627	          labels[start:end],
628	          params,
629	          opt_state,
630	      )
631	      losses.append(loss)
632	    loss = jnp.mean(jnp.array(losses))
633	    return loss, params, opt_state
634	
635	  def validate_with_minibatches(gvalues, masks, labels, inds, params):
636	    """Update params iff opt_state is not None and always returns the loss."""
637	    losses = []
638	    for start in inds:
639	      end = start + minibatch_size
640	      loss = loss_fn_jitted_val(
641	          params,
642	          detector_inputs=(gvalues[start:end], masks[start:end]),
643	          w_true=labels[start:end],
644	      )
645	      losses.append(loss)
646	    return jnp.mean(jnp.array(losses))
647	
648	  def update_fn(opt_state, params):
649	    """Updates the model parameters and returns the loss."""
650	    loss, params, opt_state = update_with_minibatches(
651	        g_values, mask, watermarked, minibatch_inds, params, opt_state
652	    )
653	    val_loss = None
654	    if g_values_val is not None:
655	      if validation_metric == ValidationMetric.TPR_AT_FPR:
656	        val_loss = update_fn_if_fpr_tpr(params)
657	      else:
658	        val_loss = validate_with_minibatches(
659	            g_values_val,
660	            mask_val,
661	            watermarked_val,
662	            minibatch_inds_val,
663	            params,
664	        )
665	
666	    return opt_state, params, loss, val_loss
667	
668	  params = detector_module.params
669	  if params is None:
670	    params = detector_module.init(param_rng, g_values[:1], mask[:1])
671	
672	  optimizer = optax.adam(learning_rate=learning_rate)
673	  opt_state = optimizer.init(params)
674	
675	  history = {}
676	  epochs_completed = 0
677	  while epochs_completed < epochs:
678	    opt_state, params, loss, val_loss = update_fn(opt_state, params)
679	    epochs_completed += 1
680	
681	    history[epochs_completed] = {
682	        "loss": loss,
683	        "val_loss": val_loss,
684	        "params": params["params"],
685	    }
686	    if verbose:
687	      if val_loss is not None:
688	        print(
689	            f"Epoch {epochs_completed}: loss {loss} (train), {val_loss} (val)"
690	        )
691	      else:
692	        print(f"Epoch {epochs_completed}: loss {loss} (train)")
693	  detector_module.params = params
694	  val_loss = np.squeeze(
695	      np.array([history[epoch]["val_loss"] for epoch in range(1, epochs + 1)])
696	  )
697	  best_val_epoch = np.argmin(val_loss) + 1
698	  min_val_loss = val_loss[best_val_epoch - 1]
699	  print(f"Best val Epoch: {best_val_epoch}, min_val_loss: {min_val_loss}")
700	  detector_module.params = {"params": history[best_val_epoch]["params"]}
701	  return history, min_val_loss
702	
703	
704	class BayesianDetector:
705	  """Bayesian detector class used for watermark detection."""
706	
707	  detector_module: BayesianDetectorModule
708	  tokenizer: Any
709	  logits_processor: logits_processing.SynthIDLogitsProcessor
710	
711	  def __init__(
712	      self,
713	      logits_processor: logits_processing.SynthIDLogitsProcessor,
714	      tokenizer: Any,
715	      params: Mapping[str, Mapping[str, Any]],
716	  ):
717	    self.detector_module = BayesianDetectorModule(
718	        watermarking_depth=len(logits_processor.keys),
719	        params=params,
720	    )
721	    self.logits_processor = logits_processor
722	    self.tokenizer = tokenizer
723	
724	  def score(self, outputs: jnp.ndarray) -> jnp.ndarray:
725	    """Score the model output for possibility of being watermarked.
726	
727	    Score is within [0, 1] where 0 is not watermarked and 1 is watermarked.
728	
729	    Args:
730	      outputs: model output of shape [batch_size, output_len]
731	
732	    Returns:
733	      scores of shape [batch_size]
734	    """
735	    # eos mask is computed, skip first ngram_len - 1 tokens
736	    # eos_mask will be of shape [batch_size, output_len]
737	    eos_token_mask = self.logits_processor.compute_eos_token_mask(
738	        input_ids=outputs,
739	        eos_token_id=self.tokenizer.eos_token_id,
740	    )[:, self.logits_processor.ngram_len - 1 :]
741	
742	    # context repetition mask is computed
743	    context_repetition_mask = (
744	        self.logits_processor.compute_context_repetition_mask(
745	            input_ids=outputs,
746	        )
747	    )
748	    # context repetition mask shape [batch_size, output_len - (ngram_len - 1)]
749	
750	    combined_mask = context_repetition_mask * eos_token_mask
751	
752	    g_values = self.logits_processor.compute_g_values(
753	        input_ids=outputs,
754	    )
755	    # g values shape [batch_size, output_len - (ngram_len - 1), depth]
756	    return self.detector_module.score(
757	        g_values.cpu().numpy(), combined_mask.cpu().numpy()
758	    )
759	
760	  @classmethod
761	  def process_raw_model_outputs(
762	      cls,
763	      *,
764	      tokenized_wm_outputs: Union[Sequence[np.ndarray], np.ndarray],
765	      tokenized_uwm_outputs: Union[Sequence[np.ndarray], np.ndarray],
766	      logits_processor: logits_processing.SynthIDLogitsProcessor,
767	      tokenizer: Any,
768	      torch_device: torch.device,
769	      test_size: float = 0.3,
770	      pos_truncation_length: Optional[int] = 200,
771	      neg_truncation_length: Optional[int] = 100,
772	      max_padded_length: int = 2300,
773	  ) -> tuple[jnp.ndarray, jnp.ndarray, jnp.ndarray, jnp.ndarray, jnp.ndarray]:
774	    """Process raw models outputs into inputs we can train.
775	
776	    Args:
777	      tokenized_wm_outputs: tokenized outputs of watermarked data.
778	      tokenized_uwm_outputs: tokenized outputs of unwatermarked data.
779	      logits_processor: logits processor used for watermarking.
780	      tokenizer: tokenizer used for the model.
781	      torch_device: torch device to use.
782	      test_size: test size to use for train-test split.
783	      pos_truncation_length: Length to truncate wm outputs. If None, no
784	        truncation is applied.
785	      neg_truncation_length: Length to truncate uwm outputs. If None, no
786	        truncation is applied.
787	      max_padded_length: Length to pad truncated outputs so that all processed
788	        entries have same shape.
789	
790	    Returns:
791	      Tuple of train_g_values, train_masks, train_labels, cv_g_values, cv_masks,
792	        cv_labels
793	    """
794	    # Split data into train and CV
795	    train_wm_outputs, cv_wm_outputs = model_selection.train_test_split(
796	        tokenized_wm_outputs, test_size=test_size
797	    )
798	
799	    train_uwm_outputs, cv_uwm_outputs = model_selection.train_test_split(
800	        tokenized_uwm_outputs, test_size=test_size
801	    )
802	
803	    # Process both train and CV data for training
804	    wm_masks_train, wm_g_values_train = process_outputs_for_training(
805	        [
806	            torch.tensor(outputs, device=torch_device, dtype=torch.long)
807	            for outputs in train_wm_outputs
808	        ],
809	        logits_processor=logits_processor,
810	        tokenizer=tokenizer,
811	        pos_truncation_length=pos_truncation_length,
812	        neg_truncation_length=neg_truncation_length,
813	        max_length=max_padded_length,
814	        is_pos=True,
815	        is_cv=False,
816	        torch_device=torch_device,
817	    )
818	    wm_masks_cv, wm_g_values_cv = process_outputs_for_training(
819	        [
820	            torch.tensor(outputs, device=torch_device, dtype=torch.long)
821	            for outputs in cv_wm_outputs
822	        ],
823	        logits_processor=logits_processor,
824	        tokenizer=tokenizer,
825	        pos_truncation_length=pos_truncation_length,
826	        neg_truncation_length=neg_truncation_length,
827	        max_length=max_padded_length,
828	        is_pos=True,
829	        is_cv=True,
830	        torch_device=torch_device,
831	    )
832	    uwm_masks_train, uwm_g_values_train = process_outputs_for_training(
833	        [
834	            torch.tensor(outputs, device=torch_device, dtype=torch.long)
835	            for outputs in train_uwm_outputs
836	        ],
837	        logits_processor=logits_processor,
838	        tokenizer=tokenizer,
839	        pos_truncation_length=pos_truncation_length,
840	        neg_truncation_length=neg_truncation_length,
841	        max_length=max_padded_length,
842	        is_pos=False,
843	        is_cv=False,
844	        torch_device=torch_device,
845	    )
846	    uwm_masks_cv, uwm_g_values_cv = process_outputs_for_training(
847	        [
848	            torch.tensor(outputs, device=torch_device, dtype=torch.long)
849	            for outputs in cv_uwm_outputs
850	        ],
851	        logits_processor=logits_processor,
852	        tokenizer=tokenizer,
853	        pos_truncation_length=pos_truncation_length,
854	        neg_truncation_length=neg_truncation_length,
855	        max_length=max_padded_length,
856	        is_pos=False,
857	        is_cv=True,
858	        torch_device=torch_device,
859	    )
860	
861	    # We get list of data; here we concat all together to be passed to the
862	    # detector.
863	    wm_masks_train = torch.cat(wm_masks_train, dim=0)
864	    wm_g_values_train = torch.cat(wm_g_values_train, dim=0)
865	    wm_labels_train = torch.ones((wm_masks_train.shape[0],), dtype=torch.bool)
866	    wm_masks_cv = torch.cat(wm_masks_cv, dim=0)
867	    wm_g_values_cv = torch.cat(wm_g_values_cv, dim=0)
868	    wm_labels_cv = torch.ones((wm_masks_cv.shape[0],), dtype=torch.bool)
869	
870	    uwm_masks_train = torch.cat(uwm_masks_train, dim=0)
871	    uwm_g_values_train = torch.cat(uwm_g_values_train, dim=0)
872	    uwm_labels_train = torch.zeros(
873	        (uwm_masks_train.shape[0],), dtype=torch.bool
874	    )
875	    uwm_masks_cv = torch.cat(uwm_masks_cv, dim=0)
876	    uwm_g_values_cv = torch.cat(uwm_g_values_cv, dim=0)
877	    uwm_labels_cv = torch.zeros((uwm_masks_cv.shape[0],), dtype=torch.bool)
878	
879	    # Concat pos and negatives data together.
880	    train_g_values = (
881	        torch.cat((wm_g_values_train, uwm_g_values_train), dim=0).cpu().numpy()
882	    )
883	    train_labels = (
884	        torch.cat((wm_labels_train, uwm_labels_train), axis=0).cpu().numpy()
885	    )
886	    train_masks = (
887	        torch.cat((wm_masks_train, uwm_masks_train), axis=0).cpu().numpy()
888	    )
889	
890	    cv_g_values = (
891	        torch.cat((wm_g_values_cv, uwm_g_values_cv), axis=0).cpu().numpy()
892	    )
893	    cv_labels = torch.cat((wm_labels_cv, uwm_labels_cv), axis=0).cpu().numpy()
894	    cv_masks = torch.cat((wm_masks_cv, uwm_masks_cv), axis=0).cpu().numpy()
895	
896	    # Free up GPU memory.
897	    del (
898	        wm_g_values_train,
899	        wm_labels_train,
900	        wm_masks_train,
901	        wm_g_values_cv,
902	        wm_labels_cv,
903	        wm_masks_cv,
904	    )
905	    gc.collect()
906	    torch.cuda.empty_cache()
907	
908	    # Shuffle data.
909	    train_g_values = jnp.squeeze(train_g_values)
910	    train_labels = jnp.squeeze(train_labels)
911	    train_masks = jnp.squeeze(train_masks)
912	
913	    cv_g_values = jnp.squeeze(cv_g_values)
914	    cv_labels = jnp.squeeze(cv_labels)
915	    cv_masks = jnp.squeeze(cv_masks)
916	
917	    shuffled_idx = list(range(train_g_values.shape[0]))
918	    shuffled_idx = np.array(shuffled_idx)
919	    np.random.shuffle(shuffled_idx)
920	    train_g_values = train_g_values[shuffled_idx]
921	    train_labels = train_labels[shuffled_idx]
922	    train_masks = train_masks[shuffled_idx]
923	
924	    shuffled_idx = list(range(cv_g_values.shape[0]))
925	    shuffled_idx = np.array(shuffled_idx)
926	    np.random.shuffle(shuffled_idx)
927	    cv_g_values = cv_g_values[shuffled_idx]
928	    cv_labels = cv_labels[shuffled_idx]
929	    cv_masks = cv_masks[shuffled_idx]
930	
931	    return (
932	        train_g_values,
933	        train_masks,
934	        train_labels,
935	        cv_g_values,
936	        cv_masks,
937	        cv_labels,
938	    )
939	
940	  @classmethod
941	  def train_best_detector_given_g_values(
942	      cls,
943	      *,
944	      train_g_values: jnp.ndarray,
945	      train_masks: jnp.ndarray,
946	      train_labels: jnp.ndarray,
947	      cv_g_values: jnp.ndarray,
948	      cv_masks: jnp.ndarray,
949	      cv_labels: jnp.ndarray,
950	      logits_processor: logits_processing.SynthIDLogitsProcessor,
951	      tokenizer: Any,
952	      n_epochs: int = 50,
953	      learning_rate: float = 2.1e-2,
954	      l2_weights: np.ndarray = np.logspace(-3, -2, num=4),
955	      verbose: bool = False,
956	  ) -> tuple["BayesianDetector", float]:
957	    """Train best detector given g_values, mask and labels."""
958	    best_detector = None
959	    lowest_loss = float("inf")
960	    val_losses = []
961	    for l2_weight in l2_weights:
962	      detector_module = BayesianDetectorModule(
963	          watermarking_depth=len(logits_processor.keys),
964	      )
965	      _, min_val_loss = train(
966	          detector_module=detector_module,
967	          g_values=train_g_values,
968	          mask=train_masks,
969	          watermarked=train_labels,
970	          g_values_val=cv_g_values,
971	          mask_val=cv_masks,
972	          watermarked_val=cv_labels,
973	          learning_rate=learning_rate,
974	          l2_weight=l2_weight,
975	          epochs=n_epochs,
976	          verbose=verbose,
977	      )
978	      val_losses.append(min_val_loss)
979	      if min_val_loss < lowest_loss:
980	        lowest_loss = min_val_loss
981	        best_detector = detector_module
982	
983	    return cls(logits_processor, tokenizer, best_detector.params), lowest_loss
984	
985	  @classmethod
986	  def train_best_detector(
987	      cls,
988	      *,
989	      tokenized_wm_outputs: Union[Sequence[np.ndarray], np.ndarray],
990	      tokenized_uwm_outputs: Union[Sequence[np.ndarray], np.ndarray],
991	      logits_processor: logits_processing.SynthIDLogitsProcessor,
992	      tokenizer: Any,
993	      torch_device: torch.device,
994	      test_size: float = 0.3,
995	      pos_truncation_length: Optional[int] = 200,
996	      neg_truncation_length: Optional[int] = 100,
997	      max_padded_length: int = 2300,
998	      n_epochs: int = 50,
999	      learning_rate: float = 2.1e-2,
1000	      l2_weights: np.ndarray = np.logspace(-3, -2, num=4),
1001	      verbose: bool = False,
1002	  ) -> tuple["BayesianDetector", float]:
1003	    """Construct, train and return the best detector based on wm and uwm data.
1004	
1005	    In practice, we have found that tuning pos_truncation_length,
1006	    neg_truncation_length, n_epochs, learning_rate and l2_weights can help
1007	    improve the performance of the detector. We recommend tuning these
1008	    parameters for your data.
1009	
1010	    Args:
1011	      tokenized_wm_outputs: tokenized outputs of watermarked data.
1012	      tokenized_uwm_outputs: tokenized outputs of unwatermarked data.
1013	      logits_processor: logits processor used for watermarking.
1014	      tokenizer: tokenizer used for the model.
1015	      torch_device: torch device to use.
1016	      test_size: test size to use for train-test split.
1017	      pos_truncation_length: Length to truncate wm outputs. If None, no
1018	        truncation is applied.
1019	      neg_truncation_length: Length to truncate uwm outputs. If None, no
1020	        truncation is done.
1021	      max_padded_length: Length to pad truncated outputs so that all processed
1022	        entries have same shape.
1023	      n_epochs: Number of epochs to train the detector.
1024	      learning_rate: Learning rate to use for training the detector.
1025	      l2_weights: L2 weights to use for training the detector.
1026	      verbose: Whether to print training progress.
1027	
1028	    Returns:
1029	      Tuple of trained detector and loss achieved on CV data.
1030	    """
1031	    if torch_device.type in ("cuda", "tpu"):
1032	      raise ValueError(
1033	          "We have found the training unstable on CPUs; we are working on"
1034	          " a fix. Use GPU or TPU for training."
1035	      )
1036	    (
1037	        train_g_values,
1038	        train_masks,
1039	        train_labels,
1040	        cv_g_values,
1041	        cv_masks,
1042	        cv_labels,
1043	    ) = cls.process_raw_model_outputs(
1044	        tokenized_wm_outputs=tokenized_wm_outputs,
1045	        tokenized_uwm_outputs=tokenized_uwm_outputs,
1046	        logits_processor=logits_processor,
1047	        tokenizer=tokenizer,
1048	        torch_device=torch_device,
1049	        test_size=test_size,
1050	        pos_truncation_length=pos_truncation_length,
1051	        neg_truncation_length=neg_truncation_length,
1052	        max_padded_length=max_padded_length,
1053	    )
1054	
1055	    return cls.train_best_detector_given_g_values(
1056	        train_g_values=train_g_values,
1057	        train_masks=train_masks,
1058	        train_labels=train_labels,
1059	        cv_g_values=cv_g_values,
1060	        cv_masks=cv_masks,
1061	        cv_labels=cv_labels,
1062	        logits_processor=logits_processor,
1063	        tokenizer=tokenizer,
1064	        verbose=verbose,

1067	        l2_weights=l2_weights,
1068	    )

# Copyright 2024 DeepMind Technologies Limited

# limitations under the License.
# ==============================================================================

"""Bayesian detector class."""

import abc
from collections.abc import Mapping, Sequence
import enum
import functools
import gc
from typing import Any, Optional, Union
import flax.linen as nn
import jax
import jax.numpy as jnp
from jaxtyping import PyTree  # pylint: disable=g-importing-member
import numpy as np
import optax
from sklearn import model_selection
import torch
import tqdm

from synthid_text import logits_processing


def pad_to_len(
    arr: torch.tensor,
    target_len: int,
    *,
    left_pad: bool,
    eos_token: int,
    device: torch.device,
) -> torch.tensor:
  """Pad or truncate array to given length."""
  if arr.shape[1] < target_len:
    shape_for_ones = list(arr.shape)
    shape_for_ones[1] = target_len - shape_for_ones[1]
    padded = (
        torch.ones(
            shape_for_ones,
            device=device,
            dtype=torch.long,
        )
        * eos_token
    )
    if not left_pad:
      return torch.concatenate((arr, padded), dim=1)
    else:
      return torch.concatenate((padded, arr), dim=1)
  else:
    return arr[:, :target_len]


def filter_and_truncate(
    outputs: torch.tensor,
    truncation_length: Optional[int],
    eos_token_mask: torch.tensor,
) -> torch.tensor:
  """Filter and truncate outputs to given length.

  Args:
   outputs: output tensor of shape [batch_size, output_len]
   truncation_length: Length to truncate the final output. If None, then no
     truncation is applied.
   eos_token_mask: EOS token mask of shape [batch_size, output_len]

  Returns:
   output tensor of shape [batch_size, truncation_length].
  """
  if truncation_length:
    outputs = outputs[:, :truncation_length]
    truncation_mask = torch.sum(eos_token_mask, dim=1) >= truncation_length
    return outputs[truncation_mask, :]
  return outputs


def process_outputs_for_training(
    all_outputs: Sequence[torch.Tensor],
    logits_processor: logits_processing.SynthIDLogitsProcessor,
    tokenizer: Any,
    *,
    pos_truncation_length: Optional[int],
    neg_truncation_length: Optional[int],
    max_length: int,
    is_cv: bool,
    is_pos: bool,
    torch_device: torch.device,
) -> tuple[Sequence[torch.tensor], Sequence[torch.tensor]]:
  """Process raw model outputs into format understandable by the detector.

  Args:
   all_outputs: sequence of outputs of shape [batch_size, output_len].
   logits_processor: logits processor used for watermarking.
   tokenizer: tokenizer used for the model.
   pos_truncation_length: Length to truncate the watermarked outputs. If None,
     then no truncation is applied.
   neg_truncation_length: Length to truncate the unwatermarked outputs. If None,
     then no truncation is applied.
   max_length: Length to pad truncated outputs so that all processed entries.
     have same shape.
   is_cv: Process given outputs for cross validation.
   is_pos: Process given outputs for positives.
   torch_device: torch device to use.

  Returns:
    Tuple of
      all_masks: list of masks of shape [batch_size, max_length].
      all_g_values: list of g_values of shape [batch_size, max_length, depth].
  """
  all_masks = []
  all_g_values = []
  for outputs in tqdm.tqdm(all_outputs):
    # outputs is of shape [batch_size, output_len].
    # output_len can differ from batch to batch.
    eos_token_mask = logits_processor.compute_eos_token_mask(
        input_ids=outputs,
        eos_token_id=tokenizer.eos_token_id,
    )
    if is_pos or is_cv:
      # filter with length for positives for both train and CV.
      # We also filter for length when CV negatives are processed.
      outputs = filter_and_truncate(
          outputs, pos_truncation_length, eos_token_mask
      )
    elif not is_pos and not is_cv:
      outputs = filter_and_truncate(
          outputs, neg_truncation_length, eos_token_mask
      )

    # If no filtered outputs skip this batch.
    if outputs.shape[0] == 0:
      continue

    # All outputs are padded to max-length with eos-tokens.
    outputs = pad_to_len(
        outputs,
        max_length,
        left_pad=False,
        eos_token=tokenizer.eos_token_id,
        device=torch_device,
    )
    # outputs shape [num_filtered_entries, max_length]

    eos_token_mask = logits_processor.compute_eos_token_mask(
        input_ids=outputs,
        eos_token_id=tokenizer.eos_token_id,
    )

    context_repetition_mask = logits_processor.compute_context_repetition_mask(
        input_ids=outputs,
    )

    # context_repetition_mask of shape [num_filtered_entries, max_length -
    # (ngram_len - 1)].
    context_repetition_mask = pad_to_len(
        context_repetition_mask,
        max_length,
        left_pad=True,
        eos_token=0,
        device=torch_device,
    )
    # We pad on left to get same max_length shape.
    # context_repetition_mask of shape [num_filtered_entries, max_length].
    combined_mask = context_repetition_mask * eos_token_mask

    g_values = logits_processor.compute_g_values(
        input_ids=outputs,
    )

    # g_values of shape [num_filtered_entries, max_length - (ngram_len - 1),
    # depth].
    g_values = pad_to_len(
        g_values, max_length, left_pad=True, eos_token=0, device=torch_device
    )

    # We pad on left to get same max_length shape.
    # g_values of shape [num_filtered_entries, max_length, depth].
    all_masks.append(combined_mask)
    all_g_values.append(g_values)
  return all_masks, all_g_values


@enum.unique
class ScoreType(enum.Enum):
  """Type of score returned by a WatermarkDetector.

  In all cases, larger score corresponds to watermarked text.
  """

  # Negative p-value where the p-value is the probability of observing equal or
  # stronger watermarking in unwatermarked text.
  NEGATIVE_P_VALUE = enum.auto()

  # Prob(watermarked | g-values).
  POSTERIOR = enum.auto()


class LikelihoodModel(abc.ABC):
  """Watermark likelihood model base class defining __call__ interface."""

  @abc.abstractmethod
  def __call__(self, g_values: jnp.ndarray) -> jnp.ndarray:
    """Computes likelihoods given g-values and a mask.

    Args:
      g_values: g-values (all are 0 or 1) of shape [batch_size, seq_len,
        watermarking_depth, ...].

    Returns:
      an array of shape [batch_size, seq_len, watermarking_depth] or
      [batch_size, seq_len, 1] corresponding to the likelihoods
      of the g-values given either the watermarked hypothesis or
      the unwatermarked hypothesis; i.e. either P(g|watermarked)
      or P(g|unwatermarked).
    """


class LikelihoodModelWatermarked(nn.Module, LikelihoodModel):
  """Watermarked likelihood model for binary-valued g-values.

  This takes in g-values and returns P(g_values|watermarked).
  """

  watermarking_depth: int
  params: Optional[Mapping[str, Mapping[str, Any]]] = None

  def setup(self):
    """Initializes the model parameters."""

    def noise(seed, shape):
      return jax.random.normal(key=jax.random.PRNGKey(seed), shape=shape)

    self.beta = self.param(
        "beta",
        lambda *x: (
            -2.5 + 0.001 * noise(seed=0, shape=(1, 1, self.watermarking_depth))
        ),
    )
    self.delta = self.param(
        "delta",
        lambda *x: (
            0.001
            * noise(
                seed=0,
                shape=(1, 1, self.watermarking_depth, self.watermarking_depth),
            )
        ),
    )

  def l2_loss(self) -> jnp.ndarray:
    return jnp.einsum("ijkl->", self.delta**2)

  def _compute_latents(
      self, g_values: jnp.ndarray
  ) -> tuple[jnp.ndarray, jnp.ndarray]:
    """Computes the unique token probability distribution given g-values.

    Args:
      g_values: Pseudorandom function values of shape [batch_size, seq_len,
        watermarking_depth].

    Returns:
      p_one_unique_token and p_two_unique_tokens, both of shape
        [batch_size, seq_len, watermarking_depth]. p_one_unique_token[i,t,l]
        gives the probability of there being one unique token in a tournament
        match on layer l, on timestep t, for batch item i.
        p_one_unique_token[i,t,l] + p_two_unique_token[i,t,l] = 1.
    """
    # Tile g-values to produce feature vectors for predicting the latents
    # for each layer in the tournament; our model for the latents psi is a
    # logistic regression model psi = sigmoid(delta * x + beta).
    x = jnp.repeat(
        jnp.expand_dims(g_values, axis=-2), self.watermarking_depth, axis=-2
    )  # [batch_size, seq_len, watermarking_depth, watermarking_depth]

    x = jnp.tril(
        x, k=-1
    )  # mask all elements above -1 diagonal for autoregressive factorization

    logits = (
        jnp.einsum("ijkl,ijkl->ijk", self.delta, x) + self.beta
    )  # [batch_size, seq_len, watermarking_depth]

    p_two_unique_tokens = jax.nn.sigmoid(logits)
    p_one_unique_token = 1 - p_two_unique_tokens
    return p_one_unique_token, p_two_unique_tokens

  def __call__(self, g_values: jnp.ndarray) -> jnp.ndarray:
    """Computes the likelihoods P(g_values|watermarked).

    Args:
      g_values: g-values (values 0 or 1) of shape [batch_size, seq_len,
        watermarking_depth]

    Returns:
      p(g_values|watermarked) of shape [batch_size, seq_len,
      watermarking_depth].
    """
    p_one_unique_token, p_two_unique_tokens = self._compute_latents(g_values)

    # P(g_tl | watermarked) is equal to
    # 0.5 * [ (g_tl+0.5) * p_two_unique_tokens + p_one_unique_token].
    return 0.5 * ((g_values + 0.5) * p_two_unique_tokens + p_one_unique_token)


class LikelihoodModelUnwatermarked(nn.Module, LikelihoodModel):
  """Unwatermarked likelihood model for binary-valued g-values.

  This takes in g-values and returns p(g_values | not watermarked).
  """

  @nn.compact
  def __call__(self, g_values: jnp.ndarray) -> jnp.ndarray:
    """Computes the likelihoods P(g-values|not watermarked).

    Args:
      g_values: g-values (0 or 1 values) of shape [batch_size, seq_len,
        watermarking_depth, ...].

    Returns:
      Likelihoods of g-values given text is unwatermarked --
      p(g_values | not watermarked) of shape [batch_size, seq_len,
      watermarking_depth].
    """
    return 0.5 * jnp.ones_like(g_values)  # all g-values have prob 0.5.


def _compute_posterior(
    likelihoods_watermarked: jnp.ndarray,
    likelihoods_unwatermarked: jnp.ndarray,
    mask: jnp.ndarray,
    prior: float,
) -> jnp.ndarray:
  """Compute posterior P(w|g) given likelihoods, mask and prior.

  Args:
    likelihoods_watermarked: shape [batch, length, depth]. Likelihoods
      P(g_values|watermarked) of g-values under watermarked model.
    likelihoods_unwatermarked: shape [batch, length, depth]. Likelihoods
      P(g_values|unwatermarked) of g-values under unwatermarked model.
    mask: A binary array shape [batch, length] indicating which g-values should
      be used. g-values with mask value 0 are discarded.
    prior: Prior probability P(w) that the text is watermarked.

  Returns:
    Posterior probability P(watermarked|g_values), shape [batch].
  """
  mask = jnp.expand_dims(mask, -1)
  prior = jnp.clip(prior, a_min=1e-5, a_max=1 - 1e-5)
  log_likelihoods_watermarked = jnp.log(
      jnp.clip(likelihoods_watermarked, a_min=1e-30, a_max=float("inf"))
  )
  log_likelihoods_unwatermarked = jnp.log(
      jnp.clip(likelihoods_unwatermarked, a_min=1e-30, a_max=float("inf"))
  )
  log_odds = log_likelihoods_watermarked - log_likelihoods_unwatermarked

  # Sum relative surprisals (log odds) across all token positions and layers.
  relative_surprisal_likelihood = jnp.einsum(
      "i...->i", log_odds * mask
  )  # [batch_size].

  relative_surprisal_prior = jnp.log(prior) - jnp.log(1 - prior)

  # Combine prior and likelihood.
  relative_surprisal = (
      relative_surprisal_prior + relative_surprisal_likelihood
  )  # [batch_size]

  # Compute the posterior probability P(w|g) = sigmoid(relative_surprisal).
  return jax.nn.sigmoid(relative_surprisal)


class BayesianDetectorModule(nn.Module):
  """Bayesian classifier for watermark detection Flax Module.

  This detector uses Bayes' rule to compute a watermarking score, which is
  the posterior probability P(watermarked|g_values) that the text is
  watermarked, given its g_values.

  Note that this detector only works with Tournament-based watermarking using
  the Bernoulli(0.5) g-value distribution.
  """

  watermarking_depth: int  # The number of tournament layers.
  params: Optional[Mapping[str, Mapping[str, Any]]] = None
  baserate: float = 0.5  # Prior probability P(w) that a text is watermarked.

  @property
  def score_type(self) -> ScoreType:
    return ScoreType.POSTERIOR

  def l2_loss(self) -> jnp.ndarray:
    return self.likelihood_model_watermarked.l2_loss()

  def setup(self):
    """Initializes the model parameters."""

    def _fetch_params():
      return {"params:": self.params["params"]["likelihood_model_watermarked"]}

    self.likelihood_model_watermarked = LikelihoodModelWatermarked(
        watermarking_depth=self.watermarking_depth,
        params=_fetch_params() if self.params is not None else None,
    )
    self.likelihood_model_unwatermarked = LikelihoodModelUnwatermarked()
    self.prior = self.param("prior", lambda *x: self.baserate, (1,))

  def __call__(
      self,
      g_values: jnp.ndarray,
      mask: jnp.ndarray,
  ) -> jnp.ndarray:
    """Computes the watermarked posterior P(watermarked|g_values).

    Args:
      g_values: g-values (with values 0 or 1) of shape [batch_size, seq_len,
        watermarking_depth, ...]
      mask: A binary array shape [batch_size, seq_len] indicating which g-values
        should be used. g-values with mask value 0 are discarded.

    Returns:
      P(watermarked | g_values), of shape [batch_size].
    """

    likelihoods_watermarked = self.likelihood_model_watermarked(g_values)
    likelihoods_unwatermarked = self.likelihood_model_unwatermarked(g_values)
    return _compute_posterior(
        likelihoods_watermarked, likelihoods_unwatermarked, mask, self.prior
    )

  def score(
      self,
      g_values: Union[jnp.ndarray, Sequence[jnp.ndarray]],
      mask: jnp.ndarray,
  ) -> jnp.ndarray:
    if self.params is None:
      raise ValueError("params must be set before calling score")
    return self.apply(self.params, g_values, mask, method=self.__call__)


def xentropy_loss(y: jnp.ndarray, y_pred: jnp.ndarray) -> jnp.ndarray:
  """Calculates cross entropy loss."""
  y_pred = jnp.clip(y_pred, 1e-5, 1 - 1e-5)
  return -jnp.mean((y * jnp.log(y_pred) + (1 - y) * jnp.log(1 - y_pred)))


def loss_fn(
    params: Mapping[str, Any],
    detector_inputs: Any,
    w_true: jnp.ndarray,
    l2_batch_weight: float,
    detector_module: BayesianDetectorModule,
) -> jnp.ndarray:
  """Calculates loss for a batch of data given parameters."""
  w_pred = detector_module.apply(
      params, *detector_inputs, method=detector_module.__call__
  )
  unweighted_l2 = detector_module.apply(params, method=detector_module.l2_loss)
  l2_loss = l2_batch_weight * unweighted_l2
  return xentropy_loss(w_true, w_pred) + l2_loss


def tpr_at_fpr(
    params: Mapping[str, Any],
    detector_inputs: Any,
    w_true: jnp.ndarray,
    minibatch_size,
    detector_module: BayesianDetectorModule,
    target_fpr: float = 0.01,
) -> jnp.ndarray:
  """Calculates TPR at FPR=target_fpr."""
  positive_idxs = w_true == 1
  negative_idxs = w_true == 0
  inds = jnp.arange(0, len(detector_inputs[0]), minibatch_size)
  w_preds = []
  for start in inds:
    end = start + minibatch_size
    detector_inputs_ = (
        detector_inputs[0][start:end],
        detector_inputs[1][start:end],
    )
    w_pred = detector_module.apply(
        params, *detector_inputs_, method=detector_module.__call__
    )
    w_preds.append(w_pred)
  w_pred = jnp.concatenate(w_preds, axis=0)
  positive_scores = w_pred[positive_idxs]
  negative_scores = w_pred[negative_idxs]
  fpr_threshold = jnp.percentile(negative_scores, 100 - target_fpr * 100)
  return jnp.mean(positive_scores >= fpr_threshold)


@enum.unique
class ValidationMetric(enum.Enum):
  """Direction along the z-axis."""

  TPR_AT_FPR = "tpr_at_fpr"
  CROSS_ENTROPY = "cross_entropy"


def train(
    *,
    detector_module: BayesianDetectorModule,
    g_values: jnp.ndarray,
    mask: jnp.ndarray,
    watermarked: jnp.ndarray,
    epochs: int = 250,
    learning_rate: float = 1e-3,
    minibatch_size: int = 64,
    seed: int = 0,
    l2_weight: float = 0.0,
    shuffle: bool = True,
    g_values_val: Optional[jnp.ndarray] = None,
    mask_val: Optional[jnp.ndarray] = None,
    watermarked_val: Optional[jnp.ndarray] = None,
    verbose: bool = False,
    validation_metric: ValidationMetric = ValidationMetric.TPR_AT_FPR,
) -> tuple[Mapping[int, Mapping[str, PyTree]], float]:
  """Trains a Bayesian detector model.

  Args:
    detector_module: The detector module to train in-place.
    g_values: g-values of shape [num_train, seq_len, watermarking_depth].
    mask: A binary array shape [num_train, seq_len] indicating which g-values
      should be used. g-values with mask value 0 are discarded.
    watermarked: A binary array of shape [num_train] indicating whether the
      example is watermarked (0: unwatermarked, 1: watermarked).
    epochs: Number of epochs to train for.
    learning_rate: Learning rate for optimizer.
    minibatch_size: Minibatch size for training. Note that a minibatch requires
      ~ 32 * minibatch_size * seq_len * watermarked_depth * watermarked_depth
      bits of memory.
    seed: Seed for parameter initialization.
    l2_weight: Weight to apply to L2 regularization for delta parameters.
    shuffle: Whether to shuffle before training.
    g_values_val: Validation g-values of shape [num_val, seq_len,
      watermarking_depth].
    mask_val: Validation mask of shape [num_val, seq_len].
    watermarked_val: Validation watermark labels of shape [num_val].
    verbose: Boolean indicating verbosity of training. If true, the loss will be
      printed. Defaulted to False.
    validation_metric: validation metric to use.

  Returns:
    Tuple of
      training_history: Training history keyed by epoch number where the
      values are
        dictionaries containing the loss, validation loss, and model
        parameters,
        keyed by
        'loss', 'val_loss', and 'params', respectively.
      min_val_loss: Minimum validation loss achieved during training.
  """
  minibatch_inds = jnp.arange(0, len(g_values), minibatch_size)
  minibatch_inds_val = None
  if g_values_val is not None:
    minibatch_inds_val = jnp.arange(0, len(g_values_val), minibatch_size)

  rng = jax.random.PRNGKey(seed)
  param_rng, shuffle_rng = jax.random.split(rng)

  def coshuffle(*args):
    return [jax.random.permutation(shuffle_rng, x) for x in args]

  if shuffle:
    g_values, mask, watermarked = coshuffle(g_values, mask, watermarked)

  def update_fn_if_fpr_tpr(params):
    """Loss function for negative TPR@FPR=1% as the validation loss."""
    tpr_ = tpr_at_fpr(
        params=params,
        detector_inputs=(g_values_val, mask_val),
        w_true=watermarked_val,
        minibatch_size=minibatch_size,
        detector_module=detector_module,
    )
    return -tpr_

  n_minibatches = len(g_values) / minibatch_size
  l2_batch_weight_train = l2_weight / n_minibatches
  l2_batch_weight_val = 0.0
  loss_fn_train = functools.partial(
      loss_fn,
      l2_batch_weight=l2_batch_weight_train,
      detector_module=detector_module,
  )
  loss_fn_jitted_val = jax.jit(
      functools.partial(
          loss_fn,
          l2_batch_weight=l2_batch_weight_val,
          detector_module=detector_module,
      )
  )

  @jax.jit
  def update(gvalues, masks, labels, params, opt_state):
    loss_fn_partialed = functools.partial(
        loss_fn_train,
        detector_inputs=(gvalues, masks),
        w_true=labels,
    )
    loss, grads = jax.value_and_grad(loss_fn_partialed)(params)
    updates, opt_state = optimizer.update(grads, opt_state)
    params = optax.apply_updates(params, updates)
    return loss, params, opt_state

  def update_with_minibatches(gvalues, masks, labels, inds, params, opt_state):
    """Update params iff opt_state is not None and always returns the loss."""
    losses = []
    for start in inds:
      end = start + minibatch_size
      loss, params, opt_state = update(
          gvalues[start:end],
          masks[start:end],
          labels[start:end],
          params,
          opt_state,
      )
      losses.append(loss)
    loss = jnp.mean(jnp.array(losses))
    return loss, params, opt_state

  def validate_with_minibatches(gvalues, masks, labels, inds, params):
    """Update params iff opt_state is not None and always returns the loss."""
    losses = []
    for start in inds:
      end = start + minibatch_size
      loss = loss_fn_jitted_val(
          params,
          detector_inputs=(gvalues[start:end], masks[start:end]),
          w_true=labels[start:end],
      )
      losses.append(loss)
    return jnp.mean(jnp.array(losses))

  def update_fn(opt_state, params):
    """Updates the model parameters and returns the loss."""
    loss, params, opt_state = update_with_minibatches(
        g_values, mask, watermarked, minibatch_inds, params, opt_state
    )
    val_loss = None
    if g_values_val is not None:
      if validation_metric == ValidationMetric.TPR_AT_FPR:
        val_loss = update_fn_if_fpr_tpr(params)
      else:
        val_loss = validate_with_minibatches(
            g_values_val,
            mask_val,
            watermarked_val,
            minibatch_inds_val,
            params,
        )

    return opt_state, params, loss, val_loss

  params = detector_module.params
  if params is None:
    params = detector_module.init(param_rng, g_values[:1], mask[:1])

  optimizer = optax.adam(learning_rate=learning_rate)
  opt_state = optimizer.init(params)

  history = {}
  epochs_completed = 0
  while epochs_completed < epochs:
    opt_state, params, loss, val_loss = update_fn(opt_state, params)
    epochs_completed += 1

    history[epochs_completed] = {
        "loss": loss,
        "val_loss": val_loss,
        "params": params["params"],
    }
    if verbose:
      if val_loss is not None:
        print(
            f"Epoch {epochs_completed}: loss {loss} (train), {val_loss} (val)"
        )
      else:
        print(f"Epoch {epochs_completed}: loss {loss} (train)")
  detector_module.params = params
  val_loss = np.squeeze(
      np.array([history[epoch]["val_loss"] for epoch in range(1, epochs + 1)])
  )
  best_val_epoch = np.argmin(val_loss) + 1
  min_val_loss = val_loss[best_val_epoch - 1]
  print(f"Best val Epoch: {best_val_epoch}, min_val_loss: {min_val_loss}")
  detector_module.params = {"params": history[best_val_epoch]["params"]}
  return history, min_val_loss


class BayesianDetector:
  """Bayesian detector class used for watermark detection."""

  detector_module: BayesianDetectorModule
  tokenizer: Any
  logits_processor: logits_processing.SynthIDLogitsProcessor

  def __init__(
      self,
      logits_processor: logits_processing.SynthIDLogitsProcessor,
      tokenizer: Any,
      params: Mapping[str, Mapping[str, Any]],
  ):
    self.detector_module = BayesianDetectorModule(
        watermarking_depth=len(logits_processor.keys),
        params=params,
    )
    self.logits_processor = logits_processor
    self.tokenizer = tokenizer

  def score(self, outputs: jnp.ndarray) -> jnp.ndarray:
    """Score the model output for possibility of being watermarked.

    Score is within [0, 1] where 0 is not watermarked and 1 is watermarked.

    Args:
      outputs: model output of shape [batch_size, output_len]

    Returns:
      scores of shape [batch_size]
    """
    # eos mask is computed, skip first ngram_len - 1 tokens
    # eos_mask will be of shape [batch_size, output_len]
    eos_token_mask = self.logits_processor.compute_eos_token_mask(
        input_ids=outputs,
        eos_token_id=self.tokenizer.eos_token_id,
    )[:, self.logits_processor.ngram_len - 1 :]

    # context repetition mask is computed
    context_repetition_mask = (
        self.logits_processor.compute_context_repetition_mask(
            input_ids=outputs,
        )
    )
    # context repetition mask shape [batch_size, output_len - (ngram_len - 1)]

    combined_mask = context_repetition_mask * eos_token_mask

    g_values = self.logits_processor.compute_g_values(
        input_ids=outputs,
    )
    # g values shape [batch_size, output_len - (ngram_len - 1), depth]
    return self.detector_module.score(
        g_values.cpu().numpy(), combined_mask.cpu().numpy()
    )

  @classmethod
  def process_raw_model_outputs(
      cls,
      *,
      tokenized_wm_outputs: Union[Sequence[np.ndarray], np.ndarray],
      tokenized_uwm_outputs: Union[Sequence[np.ndarray], np.ndarray],
      logits_processor: logits_processing.SynthIDLogitsProcessor,
      tokenizer: Any,
      torch_device: torch.device,
      test_size: float = 0.3,
      pos_truncation_length: Optional[int] = 200,
      neg_truncation_length: Optional[int] = 100,
      max_padded_length: int = 2300,
  ) -> tuple[jnp.ndarray, jnp.ndarray, jnp.ndarray, jnp.ndarray, jnp.ndarray]:
    """Process raw models outputs into inputs we can train.

    Args:
      tokenized_wm_outputs: tokenized outputs of watermarked data.
      tokenized_uwm_outputs: tokenized outputs of unwatermarked data.
      logits_processor: logits processor used for watermarking.
      tokenizer: tokenizer used for the model.
      torch_device: torch device to use.
      test_size: test size to use for train-test split.
      pos_truncation_length: Length to truncate wm outputs. If None, no
        truncation is applied.
      neg_truncation_length: Length to truncate uwm outputs. If None, no
        truncation is applied.
      max_padded_length: Length to pad truncated outputs so that all processed
        entries have same shape.

    Returns:
      Tuple of train_g_values, train_masks, train_labels, cv_g_values, cv_masks,
        cv_labels
    """
    # Split data into train and CV
    train_wm_outputs, cv_wm_outputs = model_selection.train_test_split(
        tokenized_wm_outputs, test_size=test_size
    )

    train_uwm_outputs, cv_uwm_outputs = model_selection.train_test_split(
        tokenized_uwm_outputs, test_size=test_size
    )

    # Process both train and CV data for training
    wm_masks_train, wm_g_values_train = process_outputs_for_training(
        [
            torch.tensor(outputs, device=torch_device, dtype=torch.long)
            for outputs in train_wm_outputs
        ],
        logits_processor=logits_processor,
        tokenizer=tokenizer,
        pos_truncation_length=pos_truncation_length,
        neg_truncation_length=neg_truncation_length,
        max_length=max_padded_length,
        is_pos=True,
        is_cv=False,
        torch_device=torch_device,
    )
    wm_masks_cv, wm_g_values_cv = process_outputs_for_training(
        [
            torch.tensor(outputs, device=torch_device, dtype=torch.long)
            for outputs in cv_wm_outputs
        ],
        logits_processor=logits_processor,
        tokenizer=tokenizer,
        pos_truncation_length=pos_truncation_length,
        neg_truncation_length=neg_truncation_length,
        max_length=max_padded_length,
        is_pos=True,
        is_cv=True,
        torch_device=torch_device,
    )
    uwm_masks_train, uwm_g_values_train = process_outputs_for_training(
        [
            torch.tensor(outputs, device=torch_device, dtype=torch.long)
            for outputs in train_uwm_outputs
        ],
        logits_processor=logits_processor,
        tokenizer=tokenizer,
        pos_truncation_length=pos_truncation_length,
        neg_truncation_length=neg_truncation_length,
        max_length=max_padded_length,
        is_pos=False,
        is_cv=False,
        torch_device=torch_device,
    )
    uwm_masks_cv, uwm_g_values_cv = process_outputs_for_training(
        [
            torch.tensor(outputs, device=torch_device, dtype=torch.long)
            for outputs in cv_uwm_outputs
        ],
        logits_processor=logits_processor,
        tokenizer=tokenizer,
        pos_truncation_length=pos_truncation_length,
        neg_truncation_length=neg_truncation_length,
        max_length=max_padded_length,
        is_pos=False,
        is_cv=True,
        torch_device=torch_device,
    )

    # We get list of data; here we concat all together to be passed to the
    # detector.
    wm_masks_train = torch.cat(wm_masks_train, dim=0)
    wm_g_values_train = torch.cat(wm_g_values_train, dim=0)
    wm_labels_train = torch.ones((wm_masks_train.shape[0],), dtype=torch.bool)
    wm_masks_cv = torch.cat(wm_masks_cv, dim=0)
    wm_g_values_cv = torch.cat(wm_g_values_cv, dim=0)
    wm_labels_cv = torch.ones((wm_masks_cv.shape[0],), dtype=torch.bool)

    uwm_masks_train = torch.cat(uwm_masks_train, dim=0)
    uwm_g_values_train = torch.cat(uwm_g_values_train, dim=0)
    uwm_labels_train = torch.zeros(
        (uwm_masks_train.shape[0],), dtype=torch.bool
    )
    uwm_masks_cv = torch.cat(uwm_masks_cv, dim=0)
    uwm_g_values_cv = torch.cat(uwm_g_values_cv, dim=0)
    uwm_labels_cv = torch.zeros((uwm_masks_cv.shape[0],), dtype=torch.bool)

    # Concat pos and negatives data together.
    train_g_values = (
        torch.cat((wm_g_values_train, uwm_g_values_train), dim=0).cpu().numpy()
    )
    train_labels = (
        torch.cat((wm_labels_train, uwm_labels_train), axis=0).cpu().numpy()
    )
    train_masks = (
        torch.cat((wm_masks_train, uwm_masks_train), axis=0).cpu().numpy()
    )

    cv_g_values = (
        torch.cat((wm_g_values_cv, uwm_g_values_cv), axis=0).cpu().numpy()
    )
    cv_labels = torch.cat((wm_labels_cv, uwm_labels_cv), axis=0).cpu().numpy()
    cv_masks = torch.cat((wm_masks_cv, uwm_masks_cv), axis=0).cpu().numpy()

    # Free up GPU memory.
    del (
        wm_g_values_train,
        wm_labels_train,
        wm_masks_train,
        wm_g_values_cv,
        wm_labels_cv,
        wm_masks_cv,
    )
    gc.collect()
    torch.cuda.empty_cache()

    # Shuffle data.
    train_g_values = jnp.squeeze(train_g_values)
    train_labels = jnp.squeeze(train_labels)
    train_masks = jnp.squeeze(train_masks)

    cv_g_values = jnp.squeeze(cv_g_values)
    cv_labels = jnp.squeeze(cv_labels)
    cv_masks = jnp.squeeze(cv_masks)

    shuffled_idx = list(range(train_g_values.shape[0]))
    shuffled_idx = np.array(shuffled_idx)
    np.random.shuffle(shuffled_idx)
    train_g_values = train_g_values[shuffled_idx]
    train_labels = train_labels[shuffled_idx]
    train_masks = train_masks[shuffled_idx]

    shuffled_idx = list(range(cv_g_values.shape[0]))
    shuffled_idx = np.array(shuffled_idx)
    np.random.shuffle(shuffled_idx)
    cv_g_values = cv_g_values[shuffled_idx]
    cv_labels = cv_labels[shuffled_idx]
    cv_masks = cv_masks[shuffled_idx]

    return (
        train_g_values,
        train_masks,
        train_labels,
        cv_g_values,
        cv_masks,
        cv_labels,
    )

  @classmethod
  def train_best_detector_given_g_values(
      cls,
      *,
      train_g_values: jnp.ndarray,
      train_masks: jnp.ndarray,
      train_labels: jnp.ndarray,
      cv_g_values: jnp.ndarray,
      cv_masks: jnp.ndarray,
      cv_labels: jnp.ndarray,
      logits_processor: logits_processing.SynthIDLogitsProcessor,
      tokenizer: Any,
      n_epochs: int = 50,
      learning_rate: float = 2.1e-2,
      l2_weights: np.ndarray = np.logspace(-3, -2, num=4),
      verbose: bool = False,
  ) -> tuple["BayesianDetector", float]:
    """Train best detector given g_values, mask and labels."""
    best_detector = None
    lowest_loss = float("inf")
    val_losses = []
    for l2_weight in l2_weights:
      detector_module = BayesianDetectorModule(
          watermarking_depth=len(logits_processor.keys),
      )
      _, min_val_loss = train(
          detector_module=detector_module,
          g_values=train_g_values,
          mask=train_masks,
          watermarked=train_labels,
          g_values_val=cv_g_values,
          mask_val=cv_masks,
          watermarked_val=cv_labels,
          learning_rate=learning_rate,
          l2_weight=l2_weight,
          epochs=n_epochs,
          verbose=verbose,
      )
      val_losses.append(min_val_loss)
      if min_val_loss < lowest_loss:
        lowest_loss = min_val_loss
        best_detector = detector_module

    return cls(logits_processor, tokenizer, best_detector.params), lowest_loss

  @classmethod
  def train_best_detector(
      cls,
      *,
      tokenized_wm_outputs: Union[Sequence[np.ndarray], np.ndarray],
      tokenized_uwm_outputs: Union[Sequence[np.ndarray], np.ndarray],
      logits_processor: logits_processing.SynthIDLogitsProcessor,
      tokenizer: Any,
      torch_device: torch.device,
      test_size: float = 0.3,
      pos_truncation_length: Optional[int] = 200,
      neg_truncation_length: Optional[int] = 100,
      max_padded_length: int = 2300,
      n_epochs: int = 50,
      learning_rate: float = 2.1e-2,
      l2_weights: np.ndarray = np.logspace(-3, -2, num=4),
      verbose: bool = False,
  ) -> tuple["BayesianDetector", float]:
    """Construct, train and return the best detector based on wm and uwm data.

    In practice, we have found that tuning pos_truncation_length,
    neg_truncation_length, n_epochs, learning_rate and l2_weights can help
    improve the performance of the detector. We recommend tuning these
    parameters for your data.

    Args:
      tokenized_wm_outputs: tokenized outputs of watermarked data.
      tokenized_uwm_outputs: tokenized outputs of unwatermarked data.
      logits_processor: logits processor used for watermarking.
      tokenizer: tokenizer used for the model.
      torch_device: torch device to use.
      test_size: test size to use for train-test split.
      pos_truncation_length: Length to truncate wm outputs. If None, no
        truncation is applied.
      neg_truncation_length: Length to truncate uwm outputs. If None, no
        truncation is done.
      max_padded_length: Length to pad truncated outputs so that all processed
        entries have same shape.
      n_epochs: Number of epochs to train the detector.
      learning_rate: Learning rate to use for training the detector.
      l2_weights: L2 weights to use for training the detector.
      verbose: Whether to print training progress.

    Returns:
      Tuple of trained detector and loss achieved on CV data.
    """
    if torch_device.type in ("cuda", "tpu"):
      raise ValueError(
          "We have found the training unstable on CPUs; we are working on"
          " a fix. Use GPU or TPU for training."
      )
    (
        train_g_values,
        train_masks,
        train_labels,
        cv_g_values,
        cv_masks,
        cv_labels,
    ) = cls.process_raw_model_outputs(
        tokenized_wm_outputs=tokenized_wm_outputs,
        tokenized_uwm_outputs=tokenized_uwm_outputs,
        logits_processor=logits_processor,
        tokenizer=tokenizer,
        torch_device=torch_device,
        test_size=test_size,
        pos_truncation_length=pos_truncation_length,
        neg_truncation_length=neg_truncation_length,
        max_padded_length=max_padded_length,
    )

    return cls.train_best_detector_given_g_values(
        train_g_values=train_g_values,
        train_masks=train_masks,
        train_labels=train_labels,
        cv_g_values=cv_g_values,
        cv_masks=cv_masks,
        cv_labels=cv_labels,
        logits_processor=logits_processor,
        tokenizer=tokenizer,
        verbose=verbose,
        n_epochs=n_epochs,
        learning_rate=learning_rate,
        l2_weights=l2_weights,
    )

# Copyright 2024 DeepMind Technologies Limited
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at

#    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
# ==============================================================================

"""Code for Mean and Weighted Mean scoring functions."""

from typing import Optional
import jax.numpy as jnp


def mean_score(
    g_values: jnp.ndarray,
    mask: jnp.ndarray,
) -> jnp.ndarray:
  """Computes the Mean score.

  Args:
    g_values: g-values of shape [batch_size, seq_len, watermarking_depth].
    mask: A binary array shape [batch_size, seq_len] indicating which g-values
      should be used. g-values with mask value 0 are discarded.

  Returns:
    Mean scores, of shape [batch_size]. This is the mean of the unmasked
      g-values.
  """
  watermarking_depth = g_values.shape[-1]
  num_unmasked = jnp.sum(mask, axis=1)  # shape [batch_size]
  return jnp.sum(g_values * jnp.expand_dims(mask, 2), axis=(1, 2)) / (
      watermarking_depth * num_unmasked
  )


def weighted_mean_score(
    g_values: jnp.ndarray,
    mask: jnp.ndarray,
    weights: Optional[jnp.ndarray] = None,
) -> jnp.ndarray:
  """Computes the Weighted Mean score.

  Args:
    g_values: g-values of shape [batch_size, seq_len, watermarking_depth].
    mask: A binary array shape [batch_size, seq_len] indicating which g-values
      should be used. g-values with mask value 0 are discarded.
    weights: array of non-negative floats, shape [watermarking_depth]. The
      weights to be applied to the g-values. If not supplied, defaults to
      linearly decreasing weights from 10 to 1.

  Returns:
    Weighted Mean scores, of shape [batch_size]. This is the mean of the
      unmasked g-values, re-weighted using weights.
  """
  watermarking_depth = g_values.shape[-1]

  if weights is None:
    weights = jnp.linspace(start=10, stop=1, num=watermarking_depth)

  # Normalise weights so they sum to watermarking_depth.
  weights *= watermarking_depth / jnp.sum(weights)

  # Apply weights to g-values.
  g_values *= jnp.expand_dims(weights, axis=(0, 1))

  num_unmasked = jnp.sum(mask, axis=1)  # shape [batch_size]
  return jnp.sum(g_values * jnp.expand_dims(mask, 2), axis=(1, 2)) / (
      watermarking_depth * num_unmasked
  )

1	# Copyright 2024 DeepMind Technologies Limited

14	# ==============================================================================
15	
16	"""Hashing function implementation."""
17	
18	import torch
19	
20	
21	def accumulate_hash(
22	    current_hash: torch.LongTensor,
23	    data: torch.LongTensor,
24	    multiplier: int = 6364136223846793005,
25	    increment: int = 1,
26	) -> torch.LongTensor:
27	  """Accumulate hash of data on current hash.
28	
29	  Method uses adapted linear congruential generator (LCG)with newlib/musl
30	  parameters.
31	
32	  This function has following property -
33	  f(x, data[T]) = f(f(x, data[:T - 1]), data[T])
34	
35	  This function expects current_hash.shape and data.shape[:-1] to
36	  match/broadcastable.
37	
38	  Args:
39	    current_hash: (shape,)
40	    data: (shape, tensor_len)
41	    multiplier: (int) multiplier of linear congruential generator
42	    increment: (int) increment of linear congruential generator
43	
44	  Returns:
45	    updated hash (shape,)
46	  """
47	  for i in range(data.shape[-1]):
48	    current_hash = torch.add(current_hash, data[..., i])
49	    current_hash = torch.mul(current_hash, multiplier)
50	    current_hash = torch.add(current_hash, increment)
51	  return current_hash
52
